summary: NO regression from Morse RECEIVE DSP refactor (Hann window + tonality gate in goertzel.bandToneStats + unseeded receiver decoder). /morse on preview (390x844) mounts with no red screen, no runtime error, no console error (only pre-existing shadow*/pointerEvents web-only deprecation warnings). Header, mic tap (native-only feature -> receive-unavailable-notice 'Live-Dekodierung nur im veröffentlichten Build' shown without crash), settings gear + SettingsSheet (freq-value, wpm-value present) all work. SEND flow fully re-confirmed green: 5 preset-button-1..5 render as 'Leer'; short-tap on empty preset opens PresetEditor (preset-editor-input + preset-editor-save), does NOT send; save persists as '1 CQ CQ DE HB9'; preset-repeat-1/-2/-3/-inf render when a preset is active; short-tap on filled preset-button-1 raises queue-count from '0 in Warteschlange' to '11 in Warteschlange'; selecting preset-repeat-inf then short-tap enqueues more ('23 in Warteschlange'), send-stop-button turns solid red while sending and returns queue to '0 in Warteschlange' when tapped; live typing into send-text-input increments queue ('1 in Warteschlange'); send-clear-button (Reset) clears both queue and input; presets persist across page reload (preset-button-1 still shows 'CQ CQ D...' with the ∞ badge). Conditional receive UI (receive-level-meter, receive-clear-button, receive-error-notice) is correctly NOT visible on web while not listening — no render error. Mic RECEIVE decoding itself is NATIVE-ONLY (micAvailable=false on web) so the tonality/Hann DSP change is unit-validated only; per request, we do NOT fail on that.

react_native_issues.issue: Console warnings 'shadow* style props are deprecated. Use boxShadow' and 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents' — pre-existing, non-blocking, only in web preview. No action required for this task.

critical_code_review_comments: goertzel.bandToneStats now correctly applies a Hann window (norm = 2π/(n-1)) and returns tonality = peakPower / (meanBandPower+eps), which mathematically satisfies the claimed 34/1.3/4.8 pure-tone/tap/white-noise ratios; TONALITY_ON=6 / TONALITY_OFF=3 hysteresis in useMorseReceiver is a sound rejection band for spurious taps and matches the stated fix.
useMorseReceiver correctly constructs an UNSEEDED MorseDecoder ({}, setTranscript) so decoded speed auto-locks from received elements — decouples RX from the WPM send slider as intended.
Long-silence flush uses decoderRef.current?.unitMs ?? unitMsFromWpm(seedRef.current) — auto-adapts and still has a sane fallback before the decoder locks. Good.

On the web preview autoCapitalize='characters' on send-text-input does NOT uppercase (React Native Web limitation) — this is a Web-only cosmetic mismatch, works on Android/iOS. NOT a regression from this change.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all 4 requested checks pass; 0 crashes, 0 console errors on /morse)

seed_data_creation: Ephemeral: preset 1 was populated with 'CQ CQ DE HB9' during the run and left in AsyncStorage (usePresets persistence). Reset via long-press → clear if you want a pristine state.
context_for_next_testing_agent: For /morse regression, key testIDs: header-back-button, header-title, header-mic-button, header-settings-button, receive-unavailable-notice, receive-level-meter (only while listening on native), receive-clear-button (only if transcript>0), receive-error-notice (only on native mic error), receive-transcript, preset-button-1..5, preset-editor-input, preset-editor-save, preset-editor-cancel, preset-repeat-1/-2/-3/-inf, queue-count, send-preview, send-text-input, send-stop-button, send-clear-button, settings-close-button, freq-value, wpm-value, freq-slider, wpm-slider. Mic RX decoding cannot be validated on web preview — needs a native/EAS build. DSP unit test vectors from main agent: pure 450–1200 Hz => tonality ~34; broadband tap => ~1.3; white noise => ~4.8 (both < TONALITY_ON=6, correctly rejected).
rca_of_issue: Not applicable — this iteration is a regression re-verification, not a bug fix cycle. The reported spurious-'T'/'E' from screen taps and the sporadic real-morse decode are both native-mic problems and were addressed in /app/frontend/src/morse/goertzel.ts (Hann window + tonality output) and /app/frontend/src/morse/useMorseReceiver.ts (tonality-gated on/off with hysteresis + unseeded auto-speed decoder). Verification on the web preview only confirms zero regressions to the /morse SEND/UI path; the DSP fix itself requires a native build to functionally verify with a real microphone.
